key_points: Tissues are groups of similar cells that perform a specific function
Division of labour in multicellular organisms increases efficiency
Plant tissues are classified as meristematic and permanent tissues
Meristematic tissues contain actively dividing cells responsible for growth
Permanent tissues arise from meristematic tissues through differentiation
Permanent tissues are further divided into simple and complex tissues
Animal tissues are classified into epithelial, connective, muscular and nervous tissues
examples: Apical meristem present at the tips of roots and shoots
Parenchyma cells store food in plants
Collenchyma cells provide flexibility to plant stems
Xylem transports water and minerals from roots
Phloem transports food prepared in leaves
Neuron is the structural unit of nervous tissue
formulas: Types of plant tissues → Meristematic + Permanent
Types of permanent tissues → Simple + Complex
Types of animal tissues → Epithelial + Connective + Muscular + Nervous
Parts of neuron → Cell body + Dendrites + Axon
exam_tips: Understand the difference between simple and complex permanent tissues
Remember that xylem transports water and phloem transports food
Know the four major types of animal tissues
Learn the structure of neuron for diagram questions
